US_12_04_RAILWAY_REPAIR_CONTRACT_REW: You've left no stone unturned, and the tracks are in mint condition too! Great job. The first cargo train is on its way as we speak!
US_12_04_FACTORY_METAL_CONTRACT_DESC: This old plant fell into disrepair over time, but we decided to whip it back into shape, and it's almost ready now. We just need some supplies for the repairs and a generator to get us through the initial period.
US_12_04_FACTORY_METAL_CONTRACT_REW: We're back in business, baby! The engineers and operators are at their stations, having their eardrums blasted by metallic screeching! You watch those fuel levels, and my guys will take care of the rest.
US_12_04_FACTORY_OIL_CONTRACT_DESC: Our oil well is pretty old, but it's still going strong. The issue actually lies with the refinery. We need new storage tanks and some parts to fix things up and actually ship the goods.
US_12_04_FACTORY_OIL_CONTRACT_REW: Phew! That was a job and a half, but we made it! We can now fill the new tanks with oil, and our clients can rest easy. Well, kind of...
US_12_04_OIL_DELIVERY_CONTRACT_DESC: Now that we've started processing the freshly-pumped oil, we need to have it delivered to the main port. Be careful not to spill anything, or our hydrologist friends are gonna have our heads on a platter.
US_12_04_OIL_DELIVERY_CONTRACT_REW: Nicely done! A big job means a big paycheck. The client is beyond grateful—he asked me to give you a nice strong handshake for him.
US_12_04_OBSERVATORY_CONTRACT_DESC: Our old astronomer friends like to keep up with advances in tech, so they've ordered some new solar panels and maintenance supplies. You help them out, and who knows? Maybe they'll name their next discovery after you...
US_12_04_OBSERVATORY_CONTRACT_REW: The Planet Carrier... the Delivery Guy Star System... the Trucker Asteroid... hey, how about you tell people your actual name?
US_12_04_ANTENNA_BUILDING_CONTRACT_DESC: The observatory staff needs accurate data straight from my favorite satellites, so they've decided to build additional radio telescopes closer to the facility. Are you busy right now? Any chance we could clone you?
US_12_04_ANTENNA_BUILDING_CONTRACT_REW: It's too bad we can't actually clone people, but with you around, we might not need to! Whoever said no one's irreplaceable clearly never met you...
US_12_04_LOGS_DELIVERY_CONTRACT_DESC: Since the regional renovation plan was approved, the land has been selling like hotcakes. People are eager to build houses here, so we've had a huge uptick in log orders. Good thing there's no shortage of logs...
US_12_04_LOGS_DELIVERY_CONTRACT_REW: Some folks are so tired of living in big cities, they'd go to the edge of the world to get closer to nature. We've got fresh air and nice views here—what's not to like?
US_12_04_REMOVAL_RADIOACTIVE_CONTRACT_DE: Here comes the first batch of nuclear waste! We've got a train waiting at the station to haul it away. Be careful with this cargo! We really don't want to mess up and hurt the environment.
US_12_04_REMOVAL_RADIOACTIVE_CONTRACT_RE: We can always count on you to get the job done! Time to get this nasty stuff out of here. Good riddance!
US_12_04_COOLING_TOWER_TRASH_CONTRACT_DE: The construction on the cooling towers is done, but big sites like that always get messy. We've sorted the debris into separate containers, so all you need to do is deliver them to the train station and see them off.
US_12_04_COOLING_TOWER_TRASH_CONTRACT_RE: Gotta remember to put away your toys after playtime, am I right? Anyway, the cooling towers are operational, and we've taken out the trash, which means our job in this area is done.
US_12_04_CITY_SCOUT_CONTRACT_DESC: People tell all kinds of stories about this old abandoned town. Personally, I don't believe in paranormal stuff like ghosts and the undead, but why don't you drive around and see for yourself?
US_12_04_CITY_SCOUT_CONTRACT_REW: So there are people still living there? That's wild! Good thing you didn't see any ghosts, phew... Huh? No way, I wasn't scared! More importantly, now we can demolish some of the buildings and repurpose the land.
US_12_04_FESTIVAL_PREPARING_01_TASK_DESC: We're almost done getting ready for the annual fishing festival, but we still need some supplies to fix the old boats. Do you think you'll be able to get them here on time?
US_12_04_FESTIVAL_PREPARING_01_TASK_REW: Now, we're cooking with gas! Okay everyone, take your fishing poles and let's go—time to start the festival!
US_12_04_FESTIVAL_PREPARING_02_TASK_DESC: Yup, yup, put that right here. Oh, there you are! You've been a huge help, but could you do me another favor? I need my trailer and a bunch of scrap paper brought over from the observatory. Gonna make us a bonfire!
US_12_04_FESTIVAL_PREPARING_02_TASK_REW: Thanks a ton! Hey, do you maybe wanna join the festival? I've got a couple beers with your name on 'em in the cooler...
US_12_04_BAD_PARKING_PLACE_TASK_DESC: I always back my jeep right up to the boats to make loading easier, but it looks like the river bank eroded this time, and my car ended up sinking. Can you help me pull it out and tow it to my house?
US_12_04_BAD_PARKING_PLACE_TASK_REW: My baby's okay, phew! She's got some scratches on her, but beggars can't be choosers. Thanks a bunch!
US_12_04_TRAIL_TO_THE_TOWER_TASK_DESC: This bridge was pretty worn down as it is, and the latest tremors were the final straw. This was the shortest way to the watchtower, too. Can we count on you?
US_12_04_TRAIL_TO_THE_TOWER_TASK_REW: And now traffic is restored. Not that the trail has gotten any easier, but at least we can get to the watchtower now.
US_12_04_OBSTACLE_ON_THE_ROAD_TASK_DESC: Earth tremors aren't uncommon in this region, but Mother Nature outdid herself when she dumped a landslide on the old town bypass. Could you help us move the rubble?
US_12_04_OBSTACLE_ON_THE_ROAD_TASK_REW: Much better. You know, I don't believe in ghosts, so I actually love coming to the old town when I need some “me” time. You should try it when you get the chance!
US_12_04_SLIPPERY_ROAD_TASK_DESC: Hey friend, can you tow a truck for us? The driver decided to take a shortcut but lost control of his vehicle. Luckily, he wasn't hurt too bad, but that truck is a no-go...
US_12_04_SLIPPERY_ROAD_TASK_REW: We really appreciate this. Thanks a ton, and be careful out there! Don't repeat our mistakes.
US_12_04_OIL_PROBLEMS_TASK_DESC: There was just an accident involving a fuel truck. Some of the gas leaked into the river, and the scientists are furious now! We need to get the truck out right away and assess the level of contamination.
US_12_04_OIL_PROBLEMS_TASK_REW: The rest is up to the scientists, but jeez was that rough! In any case, you did your part, so thanks a ton.
US_12_04_WRONG_TURN_TASK_DESC: Hey bud, could you possibly tow our car to the old town? My friends and I were planning to explore it, but we took a detour to that pretty lake, and the rest is history. We could actually use some repairs, too...
US_12_04_WRONG_TURN_TASK_REW: You're a life-saver! I was about ready to kiss our plans goodbye. We definitely didn't budget for this, but it's still better than losing the car...
US_12_04_HIGHWAY_TASK_DESC: We want to finish the construction project, even though the company basically hijacked our truck. We're short on time but there's a lot to do, meaning we need more manpower, workers' cabins, and spare parts.
US_12_04_HIGHWAY_TASK_REW: That's better! Things should speed up now. How do you like the truck, by the way? If you don't need it anymore, maybe you could...?
US_12_04_LOGS_PROBLEM_TASK_DESC: Yeah, I know, the roads are awful here. We're working on restoring the region, and you've been a great help. But uh, you know... We had a trailer full of logs, and now we need it towed...
US_12_04_LOGS_PROBLEM_TASK_REW: It's all here, perfect! By the way, do you happen to need any workers' cabins?
US_12_04_SMALL_HELP_TASK_DESC: We're planning to remodel our home. We really need construction materials. Ordinary delivery drivers can't make it up here, but you are a special driver. Could you help us?
US_12_04_SMALL_HELP_TASK_REW: We can finally renovate our rooms. Here's to your work!
US_12_04_UNFINISHED_BUSINESS_TASK_DESC: An incident happened on the job. My truck got stuck on the road. I had to abandon the trailer, and barely made it out with just the tractor unit. If you can help me with the trailer, I'll pay you handsomely.
US_12_04_UNFINISHED_BUISNESS_TASK_REW: I won't go there again, I swear! You helped me out, and I, in turn, will help you out with this little bonus.
US_12_04_FOREST_VECHICLE_TASK_DESC: We made camp and spent a few days in this beautiful place. Then I decided to head into the city for groceries, but I took a slightly different way back. Now the car is in a ditch, but I somehow emerged unscathed. Could you help me pull it out?
US_12_04_FOREST_VECHICLE_TASK_REW: Now we can return from this vacation, you have my gratitude and a nice little bonus for your help!
US_12_04_USEFUL_SEMITRAILER_TASK_DESC: As soon as I stepped on the gas, the semi-trailer broke free! It rolled over the edge and fell down the slop. Listen, help me get it back.
US_12_04_USEFUL_SEMITRAILER_TASK_REW: That's better. thanks for your help! You know what's an ever better idea? If you take it for yourself! I don't think it will give you any trouble at all.
US_12_04_COASTLINE_RACE_CONTEST_DESC: We've got another challenge for you! How fast can you travel the coastline? It's not your usual route—lots of water and rocks; people almost never go there. You up for some thrills?
US_12_04_COASTLINE_RACE_CONTEST_REW: It's like you were gliding over the water on a jet ski, not even driving a car! Zoom-zoom!
RU_13_01_AERODROM_01_CONTRACT_DESC: Time may be the great equalizer, but all great things come from small beginnings. Take us, for example: we need concrete to restore the main part of the building. After that, we're going to need all the construction debris hauled away. Think you can handle it?
RU_13_01_AERODROM_01_CONTRACT_REW: Alright, we're off to a great start! The main building's already been restored, so we're halfway to getting the airport up and running again. Keep up the good work!
RU_13_01_AERODROM_02_CONTRACT_DESC: Before we can get the airport running again, we need to repair the flight control deck and the unloading area. We'll need metal sheets and some containers to make it happen. We're almost to the finish line!
RU_13_01_AERODROM_02_CONTRACT_REW: Raqmet sizge! That means "thank you" in Kazakh. You've helped us get our planes flying again, and that's made life around here a whole lot easier.
RU_13_01_SUPPLY_CAREER_01_CONTRACT_DESC: Hey, a new worker! We need as much help as we can get right now. To make a long story short, we're still getting the new quarries sorted out, and we haven't had time to move the equipment from the old quarry to the logistics center yet. It shouldn't take more than twenty minutes or so, just a quick trip. Would you mind?
RU_13_01_SUPPLY_CAREER_01_CONTRACT_REW: Wow, that was great! Thanks a million! Now we can start worrying about whether our gear even works or not. Anyway, time to get to work!
RU_13_01_SUPPLY_CAREER_02_CONTRACT_DESC: Listen, pal... remember the equipment you hauled to the logistics center? Well, my boss wants us to move it to the new silver quarry now. Do me a solid, would ya?
RU_13_01_SUPPLY_CAREER_02_CONTRACT_REW: Perfect timing! Our guys were just getting ready for the next shift, and this equipment will really help them with some of the tougher work. We owe you one!
RU_13_01_SUPPLY_CAREER_03_CONTRACT_DESC: The tank turned over in the quarry! Without fuel, workers can lose their minds! Urgently, take the damaged tank to the logistics center. And the new tank with barrels needs to be picked up at the gas station and distributed to the quarries.
RU_13_01_SUPPLY_CAREER_03_CONTRACT_REW: I've lived in this godforsaken place for a long time now, driven by a single instinct: survival. Uh... what was I talking about? Never mind. You're the most dedicated guy I've ever met. Thank for your help.
RU_13_01_SUPPLY_CAREER_04_CONTRACT_DESC: The quarry's going full blast now! Well, almost... we're still missing a few things: a new drill and some parts for it. They've already been delivered to the airport, but we still need to have them shipped here so we can try them out.
RU_13_01_SUPPLY_CAREER_04_CONTRACT_REW: Whoa, check this puppy out! It looks like it'll cut through rock like a hot knife through butter. We're about to slap it together and find out. We couldn't have done it without you!
RU_13_01_LOGISTICS_CENTER_CONTRACT_DESC: We were supposed to get some new parts from town: two filters boxes, a filters box large, a compressor with extra power, a box of batteries, and a large burner. The delivery's been delayed. Do you think you could grab it for us?
RU_13_01_LOGISTICS_CENTER_CONTRACT_REW: Now that's what I call a speedy delivery! Here's your cut, and a little extra on top! But don't forget to button up next time, okay?
RU_13_01_CONSTRUCTION_RIBBONS_01_CONTRAC: Lousy piece of junk! The conveyor belt broke down in two places at the same time! We've got a big order to fill, and we need to act fast. We need spare parts and metal rods. Can you deliver the entire order to these coordinates?

Aright, the belt's as good as new now! All the holes are patched, and the connection to the plant's been reestablished. Now the silver will be delivered right to the plant. Thank you so much for your help!
RU_13_01_CONSTRUCTION_RIBBONS_02_CONTRAC: Cloudy with a Chance of Rocks

This conveyor belt used to work like gangbusters. And it still does, as long as it's run by people who know what they're doing... and have plenty of parts and metal rods, of course. You deliver the parts, we'll get the old girl back on her feet. Then we'll be off to the races!
There we go! No matter how reliable your gear might seem, you still need to do regular maintenance on it — unless you want to get beaned by a chunk of rock, anyway. Well, here's your cut.
RU_13_01_EXPORT_SHALE_01_CONTRACT_DESC: We're hard at work getting production up and running again. It's going pretty well, but we're short on slate. It just so happens that they've got a batch of slate ready to ship out over at the slate quarry. We literally just need a couple of little rocks. Would you mind?
RU_13_01_EXPORT_SHALE_01_CONTRACT_REW: Now that's what I call "a couple of little rocks!" I never thought they'd be this big. We've got more than enough slate now — but you can never have too much, right? Thanks a lot for delivering it. Here's a reward for all your hard work.
RU_13_01_EXPORT_SHALE_02_CONTRACT_DESC: To make a long story short, pal, we've got a big order, and our client doesn't mess around. We need you to haul a couple of big blocks to the airport, and we really want to see how fast you can get the job done. Alright, off you go!
RU_13_01_EXPORT_SHALE_02_CONTRACT_REW: Way to go, pal! You really nailed that one. The client's happy, and the order's already getting loading onto the plane. Here's your cut, as well as a little bonus for your professionalism.
RU_13_01_REPAIR_SILVER_CONTRACT: In the Ruins of Former Glory
RU_13_01_REPAIR_SILVER_CONTRACT_DESC: The silver plant is all set to start running again. We just need to repair a couple of fitting assembles, but we can't do it without some special parts. Can we count on you to deliver them?
RU_13_01_REPAIR_SILVER_CONTRACT_REW: Alright, everything's running like a Swiss watch now! Man, I'd love to go to Switzerland some day... there I go daydreaming again. Alright, let's fire the plant up!
RU_13_01_DELIVERY_TROLLEY_CONTRACT_DESC: Things are running pretty smoothly now! After we got the factory running, my boss decided to replace some of the old mine carts to speed things up a bit. The only problem is that they still need to be shipped to the quarry from the plant. Think you can help us out?
RU_13_01_DELIVERY_TROLLEY_CONTRACT_REW: Whoa, check out these sweet new carts! They're brand new, straight from the factory. I bet things'll go a lot more smoothly with these babies around. I wonder if we could pimp these rides a little more... eh, never mind. Here's your reward!
RU_13_01_EXPORT_SILVER_CONTRACT: The Treasury is Empty, My Lord
RU_13_01_EXPORT_SILVER_CONTRACT_DESC: Here's the first batch of processed silver. We need to deliver it to the airport and unload it. I hope you realize how important this cargo is... so please don't lose it or wander off with it. If all goes well, we'll pay you a pretty penny. Sound good?
RU_13_01_EXPORT_SILVER_CONTRACT_REW: We always appreciate your help delivering our precious cargo. Payment should always be commensurate with risk, so here's your cut. Pleasure doing business with you!
RU_13_01_GEO_EXPLORATION_CONTRACT_DESC: In addition to mining, we also need to predict the future movements of rock strata. Scanning the area with a special radar could help with this. The important thing is to find the right place to put the radar.
RU_13_01_GEO_EXPLORATION_CONTRACT_REW: That was a tremendous scouting operation! This is the perfect spot for the radar. We just need to get it here, and then we'll be golden! Oh, here's your reward for the valuable data.
RU_13_01_SEISMO_01_CONTRACT_DESC: The old system of underground mine shafts could cave in because of the work we've been doing in the new deposits, so we need to get some information about the current condition of these abandoned shafts. Feel like testing the soil for sturdiness?
RU_13_01_SEISMO_01_CONTRACT_REW: The support structures passed the test, so the risk of internal collapse is extremely low. Nevertheless, we've already submitted a request to shut the mine shafts down. Thanks for your help!
RU_13_01_SEISMO_02_CONTRACT_DESC: To stop kids from playing in the new slate quarry, we've been telling them there's a terrifying creature living at the bottom that'll take them away. But, thanks to our safety regulations, we still need to make sure the rock stratum is stable. Would you mind checking it out?
RU_13_01_SEISMO_02_CONTRACT_REW: Well, the thumper knocked a bunch of loose pebbles into the quarry, but the big rocks are still firmly in place. That means we can get back to work without having to worry. Here's your payment.
RU_13_01_SEISMO_03_CONTRACT_DESC: Silver is a valuable thing, so silver mining has to be done quickly. The new silver quarry is full of cavities that could lead to a cave-in, so we need to quickly check the rock stratum to make sure it's secure. Can you do it?
RU_13_01_SEISMO_03_CONTRACT_REW: Good job! Judging from the results of your test, there aren't very many cavities in the soil, but we'll need to build additional supports as time goes on. I'd like to pay you in silver, but I'm afraid all we've got is money, so here you go.
RU_13_01_HELP_ADMINISTRATION_01_DESC: Welcome to our town! We're having some issues with the power right now, and we're almost out of candles, but the mining company has agreed to lend us a couple of generators. Would you mind grabbing them from the logistics center?
RU_13_01_HELP_ADMINISTRATION_01_REW: These outages have already cost us a ton of candles. But the power's back on now, and the locals really appreciate it. By the way, we just need you to sign a few forms... here, and here... great. Alright, here's your pay.
RU_13_01_HELP_ADMINISTRATION_02_DESC: We just got a batch of bricks for repairing unsafe apartment buildings. We need them delivered to the processing workshop in the village. It's urgent, and we don't have enough drivers or vehicles. We need your help!
RU_13_01_HELP_ADMINISTRATION_02_REW: Great news — we can start repairing the apartment buildings very soon! You really helped us out of a jam here. You deserve a reward for everything you've done. If you could just sign here...
RU_13_01_HELP_ADMINISTRATION_03_DESC: The inspector found damage to the buildings' concrete, so it's going to take more than just bricks to fix the problem. The concrete factory has sent us a couple of plates. Now we just need to have the local craftsmen saw them into blocks and deliver them to town.
RU_13_01_HELP_ADMINISTRATION_03_REW: Great! We've got the resources we need, and the construction crew is ready to go, so now it's time to breath some new life into these buildings. Well done! We'll just need you to sign a few bills of lading...
RU_13_01_HUMANITARIAN_ASSISTANCE_FOOD_DE: Greetings, traveler! We don't get many visitors around here. But since you're here, would you mind helping us out? The government can't give us any vehicles to deliver food to the camps, and our supplies are running out. We won't haggle about the price!
RU_13_01_HUMANITARIAN_ASSISTANCE_FOOD_RE: On behalf of all the nomads, we thank you for helping us in our hour of need. If you ever want to break bread with us or just relax after a long journey, you're always welcome here.
RU_13_01_HUMANITARIAN_ASSISTANCE_FUEL_DE: Thanks for stopping by, pal! Here's the thing... we really need some gas, or pretty soon we won't be able to run our generators anymore. And without them, things will start going south around here in a hurry. We'll owe you one!
RU_13_01_HUMANITARIAN_ASSISTANCE_FUEL_RE: Long live the internal combustion engine! Let there be light, warmth, and a little bit of electricity in our yurts! Thanks for delivering the gas. If you ever feel like staying here, you're more than welcome. We'll always find a place for you.
RU_13_01_HUMANITARIAN_ASSISTANCE_WATER_D: It's great to see you! You'll never believe what happened — the local water truck's engine died, and now it's just sitting idle. We're running out of water, and without it, we're in big trouble. Would you help us out and bring us some water from the local water tower?
RU_13_01_HUMANITARIAN_ASSISTANCE_WATER_R: You're the best! You really saved our bacon. Now we don't need to worry about our people or livestock suffering from thirst. We can even take a shower at the end of a long workday! Here's your pay for helping us out.
RU_13_01_WATER_TOWER_REPAIR_DESC: The pipes are in pretty rough shape. Aside from this old water tower, there's nowhere else to get water. As always, the pipes burst, time flies, and the water's all gone. We gotta fix it. Don't worry about the pay — the whole village pitched in for this one.
RU_13_01_WATER_TOWER_REPAIR_REW: Wow, that was fast! The new pipes are hooked up, the seams have been checked and double-checked, and the valves are properly installed. We can turn the water back on now! Here's your cut. I'm gonna go wet my whistle — I'm dry as a bone over here!
RU_13_01_AVIATION_FUEL_TASK_DESC: Come in! Airfield dispatch here. We're in urgent need of fuel! If we don't get some soon, we'll need to start canceling flights. We require assistance ASAP. Repeat, we require assistance ASAP! Awaiting delivery from the nearest fuel station. Over and out.
RU_13_01_AVIATION_FUEL_TASK_REW: Come in! Airfield dispatch here. Both runways are operating normally. There are no changes to the flight schedule. And it's all thanks to you! Happy trails! Over and out.
RU_13_01_LOGS_PROBLEM: Cranes, Saws, and Three Smoking Logs
RU_13_01_LOGS_PROBLEM_TASK_DESC: These are top-notch evergreen trees — raised in Siberia, chopped down in Zherbaisk, and as long as a five-story building. But what's the point? They're just lying there rotting away while we wait for the crane operator to start his shift. Would you mind hitching them to your truck and delivering them to the village?
RU_13_01_LOGS_PROBLEM_TASK_REW: Great! Now that the logs have been delivered, the villagers will take over. Our job is done! I'm just glad the logs got delivered before they rotted away. They'll last a long time now!
RU_13_01_LOGS_FOR_HOUSE_DESC: My God, it's a good thing nobody got hurt. The cable barely hung on — it's a wonder it didn't snap. It sure took a pounding, though. I'm gonna need some help to get my vehicle out of here. It's stuck pretty good. Could you give me a hand?
RU_13_01_LOGS_FOR_HOUSE_REW: Thanks for the assist! It's a good thing the logs are sturdy and didn't crack during the accident — that means they'll make a fine building. Anyway, safe travels. Here's your cut as payment.
RU_13_01_SHEPHERD_TROUBLE_DESC: Man, this was a hell of a car back in the day! 92 horsepower, light, easy to drive... and now all she's good for is scrap. Think you could tow her to the garage for me? It'll be way easier for me to fix her up there.
RU_13_01_SHEPHERD_TROUBLE_REW: She's alive! I can't believe my baby just started right up! I'm still gonna have to replace a lot more parts to get her drive properly... anyway, here's a little something from my "rainy day fund".
RU_13_01_HELP_BORAT_DESC: They're about to start a new construction project at the helicopter pad, and, as you can see, the crew trailer we ordered has been held up. I'd hate to leave the workers without a roof over their heads, but I also can't get there on my own. Could you give me a lift?
RU_13_01_HELP_BORAT_REW: Hey, you really saved my neck. At least the workers at the helicopter pad will be warm now. Here's some of my pay — you earned it.
RU_13_01_NOT_A_ROLLER_COASTER_DESC: Of all the rotten luck! I was hoping to shorten my route to the quarry by cutting through this ditch. I calculated all the risks carefully, but I suck at math, so now I'm stuck. Since you're here, would you mind giving me a hand?
RU_13_01_NOT_A_ROLLER_COASTER_REW: I sure was lucky you drove by! I got away with a warning instead of a ticket. By the way, in case anybody asks you about it, we put your name on the insurance for this truck...
RU_13_01_TOURIST_LIST_DESC: My buddy and me decided to start traveling in our old age. It's a good thing we made up high — there was a landslide last night that damaged our vehicle. We ain't exactly spring chickens — we're not about to push that thing all the way to town. Feel like giving a couple of old geezers a tow?
RU_13_01_TOURIST_LIST_REW: Thanks for helping us out with the vehicle. When you get old, you start to appreciate life more, and no amount of money can ever give you back the time you lost. So here's a little token of my gratitude for all your time and hard work.
RU_13_01_GEO_RADAR_DESC: This place is perfect for scanning! I've already submitted a request for the equipment, and the radar is waiting at the logistics center. I just need someone to deliver it, and then I can turn it on and start scanning. Well begun is half done, am I right?
RU_13_01_GEO_RADAR_REW: Okay, everything's underway, and the sensor's operating within normal parameters. Yup... everything's looking good so far. We'll see what the results are at the end of the scan! Here's a little present on behalf of the entire Geological Society. Thank you for all your hard work!
RU_13_01_NORTHERN_BRIDGE_DESK: This bridge didn't make it through the last landslide, so it's out of commission at the moment. It hung on as long as it could, but nature had the last laugh. Looks like we gotta fix it. You in?
RU_13_01_NORTHERN_BRIDGE_REW: Nice work! This route will definitely make life easier for the locals, since they don't have to go all the way around anymore. And the bridge is sturdier than ever.
RU_13_01_EASTERN_BRIDGE_DESK: This bridge served the people of this village for a long, long time. But the soil shifted due to the mining operation, and the bridge collapsed. The villagers have saved up enough to fix it, but they still need to have the necessary resources delivered.
RU_13_01_EASTERN_BRIDGE_REW: You've made the villagers' lives a whole lot easier — they can finally use the bridge to head south. The bridge is open to anyone who wants to use it. Thank you for your help!
RU_13_01_QUARRY_BLOCKAGE_DESK: Dammit, some stone blocks fell off the side! They're blocking the other way out of the quarry now. We need to get this sorted in a hurry. It shouldn't take more than a couple hours — you just need to bring us some metal rods and parts.
RU_13_01_QUARRY_BLOCKAGE_REW: Okay, the blockage has been cleared, and this shouldn't happen again. Even if there's an earthquake, this stone wall will definitely hold — I guarantee it! I appreciate your efficiency, especially on the financial level.
RU_13_01_CANYON_BLOCKAGE_DESK: This is bad... I can't get reach the emergency relief service! There's barely any signal here anyway. Looks like we're on our own. I'll get the workers together while you bring us some parts for clearing the landslide. We'll settle up afterwards, okay?
RU_13_01_CANYON_BLOCKAGE_REW: Great! Now that the path is clear, we can drive down this road without worrying about getting brained by falling rocks — to the extent that's possible, anyway. This place is definitely safe now. I mean... probably.
RU_13_01_GREAT_DEAL_DESC: Jeep for sale! For parts/not working. Doesn't start. Been outside for a long time. Needs new engine, power train, and gearbox. Reasonable offers considered.
RU_13_01_GREAT_DEAL_REW: I never thought the old girl would start again! It gladdens my heart to see it. Go ahead, take her. That hunk of junk wasn't worth a dime before, and I can't pay for the repairs anyway. I hope she serves you well.
RU_13_01_TOWER_ROAD_SCOUT_TASK: All Roads Lead to the Tower
RU_13_01_TOWER_ROAD_SCOUT_TASK_DESC: Our central tower has an excellent view of the area. I recommend checking it out. And while you're at it, you can evaluate the condition of the road. Is it passable for tourists? I'm not asking you to do it for free, of course!
RU_13_01_TOWER_ROAD_SCOUT_TASK_REW: Yeah, it'd be hard to drive cars on the road, but all in all, it's pretty okay — no landslides or anything, and the potholes are only on the other side of the tower. Here's a little something for that eagle eye of yours.
RU_13_01_RIBBONS_ROAD_SCOUT_TASK_DESC: Sometimes double-checking something just isn't enough! So would you mind doing me a little favor? I need you to take a drive around the conveyor belt's connecting points and make sure they look alright... just in case.
RU_13_01_RIBBONS_ROAD_SCOUT_TASK_REW: Now I can finally sleep at night! As far as I can tell, the mechanical connections aren't as worn out in the other places, so they're working just fine. Thanks again! Here's some compensation for your trouble.
RU_13_01_SOUTH_ROAD_SCOUT_TASK_DESC: We don't use the detour that often, but the asphalt road to the plant is really worn out. The rock also isn't particularly stable thanks to all the mining. Could you check the route?
RU_13_01_SOUTH_ROAD_SCOUT_TASK_REW: Yeah, the route's more dangerous than it used to be — there have been at least a couple more landslides, and the swampy area is spreading. But the main groove is still there, so we can sort the rest out. Thanks for inspecting it. Here's your pay.
RU_13_01_WATER_SHARES_STONE_TASK_DESC: I heard you helped the locals fix the water tower. Well, we could use some help with water in the quarries too. We've already got a tank all ready for you. You just need to go to the old quarry and pick it up.
RU_13_01_WATER_SHARES_STONE_TASK_REW: Way to go! We've got water for days now! I could definitely switch from showers to baths now. But all we've got is buckets, so... anyway, thanks for the help!
RU_13_01_TRASH_EXPORT_TASK_DESC: Your environment can really affect your mood, you know? Well, we've got a whole bunch of trash piled up from all the work we've been doing, and it's made working here pretty unpleasant. Cold you lug all this junk to the recycling center for us?
RU_13_01_TRASH_EXPORT_TASK_REW: Now that's more like it! There's nothing like being surrounded by cleanliness and order as far as the eye can see. It makes you think more clearly and work more efficiently. Here's your pay. Thanks for your help!
RU_13_01_RELIABLE_SUPPORT_TASK_DESC: The only true constant is change, and that goes double for support structures in mines — they're always getting swapped out. So we need new beams to make sure there isn't a cave-in in either the silver quarry or the slate quarry.
RU_13_01_RELIABLE_SUPPORT_TASK_REW: Hey, you're just in the nick of time — our workers were starting to notice more and more cracks in the wooden struts, and the metal beams were getting rusty too. Well, our guys are already on it. Thanks for giving us a hand.
RU_13_01_IN_SEARCH_OF_AIDAHAR_CONTEST_DE: The Aidakhar is a wily creature indeed! I've been after it for twenty years now. They say it's been spotted in the lakes here at night, but I need to be certain! The faster you can search the coastline, the more chances you'll have to catch it!
RU_13_01_IN_SEARCH_OF_AIDAHAR_CONTEST_RE: Holy cow, that was fast! I saw the traffic lights blinking in the distance, and you were already gone! Anyway, you gotta tell me — did you see the beast, or are the villagers just telling tall tales out of boredom?
RU_13_01_MOUNTAIN_CLIMBING_CONTEST_DESC: Hey, traveler, we have a little contest here every year to see who can make it up and down the mountain paths the fastest. Anybody can enter, by the way. Should I sign you up?
RU_13_01_MOUNTAIN_CLIMBING_CONTEST_REW: Wow, you took off like a wild mustang! There was nothing left but a cloud of dust. You got a great time too. Here's your prize!
US_14_01_SCOUT_FARM_DESC: I see you're all ready to go, but first things first — we need to see what we're working with. Could you check the farms in the area for us? We need to get a sense of the extent of the damage. We'll pay you after you hand in your report.
US_14_01_SCOUT_FARM_REW: Well, there's plenty of work to do, but it shouldn't be a problem for someone with your experience. Speaking of, you can take our vehicle if you need it. It might be more efficient.
US_14_01_HAY_COLLECTION_DESC: Hey there! I'm in a bit of a bind here. My son is in Vienna for a couple of weeks, and I'm worried our hay could rot by the time he gets back thanks to all this rain. Please help me till and harvest the field. I'm too old to do it alone.
US_14_01_HAY_COLLECTION_REW: You've done me a solid, young man! I've got a bit of an emergency stash in my pill box. Here, you can have some of it. By the way, could you also get me a glass of water before you leave?
US_14_01_SOY_DELIVERY_DESC: Our farms have been getting a lot of orders for soybeans, but the latest shipment of seeds didn't arrive on time 'cuz of all the rain. Transportation issues shouldn't stop our customers from getting their goods. Could you help us out?
US_14_01_SOY_DELIVERY_REW: Thanks a bunch! We can finally plant a new batch of soybeans now! We're lucky to have understanding customers who were willing to give us a little more time. Sure, we had to offer them a discount, but it's still worth it. And here's your cut!
US_14_01_HELP_FROM_THE_PAST_DESC: A little while ago I noticed an old, rusted-out tractor in the marsh. I think it could serve us well if we retrieved it and fixed it up. We can't do it without some additional equipment, but Elias happens to have the right trailer for the job.
US_14_01_HELP_FROM_THE_PAST_REW: What a beaut! Just look at those treads—and the body! Yeah... it's the spitting image of the tractor my grampa lost to some Mr. Swamp back in the day. Well, whatever. It's yours now!
US_14_01_FARM_SOYA_DESC: When it rains, it pours... we can't get a hold of our farmhands Tucker and Dale. I sure hope those two are okay, but we can't wait around until they show up. Could you give us a hand? We'll pay for your trouble.
US_14_01_FARM_SOYA_REW: Man, am I ever glad I met you! You're a real jack of all trades. Good job! By the way, my guys came back. They're both fine, they just ran into a spot of trouble out in the boonies. But never mind that. Here's the money I promised you.
US_14_01_FARM_POTATO_DESC: Hello there. The name's Willie. Joe, Albert, and I have been planting potatoes for decades now, but we're not getting any younger, and we could use some help. Would you mind doing some field work for us?
US_14_01_FARM_POTATO_REW: Well, as we country folk say, planting potatoes is way easier that robbing banks. Here's your cut of the money. Don't mind the crumpled bills—they're definitely the real deal.
US_14_01_LOST_LARGE_CARGO_DESC: The owner of the castle placed a top-secret order and asked us to keep an eye on it, but then the damn flood came, and everything just washed away. Please help me find the containers before he decides to order a guillotine...
US_14_01_LOST_LARGE_CARGO_REW: There it is, finally! The containers look intact. Good job! Here's hoping they don't contain anything fragile, or heads are gonna roll. Well, here's your reward.
US_14_01_REPAIR_AREA_DESC: Harvest season is upon us, which means we need to stay on top of tractor repairs. But with all this rain, we just couldn't get ready in time. Could you help us out with the fuel and check on a shipment for us? The delivery is running late for some reason.
US_14_01_REPAIR_AREA_REW: I'm so glad you agreed to help! We wouldn't have the equipment we need if it not for you, but now the tractors are ready to be repaired and put to work. Thanks for the assist!
US_14_01_EMERGENCY_HELP_DESC: The bridge is almost ready, although the guys on one end are out of supplies, and the guys on the other need special equipment. Start with whatever you want, but this bridge needs to be finished. Can we count on you to deliver both?
US_14_01_EMERGENCY_HELP_REW: Thanks for your help! The work is going just fine now that we have both the supplies and the tools. Your efforts deserve a reward.
US_14_01_WAREHOUSE_PROBLEMS_DESC: Looks like our warehouse was struck by lightning! We need to have it replaced. Could you dismantle whatever's left and help us build a new one?
US_14_01_WAREHOUSE_PROBLEMS_REW: Good job! The new warehouse is ready to go. Weirdly enough, the lightning struck a metal dumpster. We should take out the trash more often... and maybe invest in some lightning rods. Anyway, here's your pay.
US_14_01_STARTING_WORK_DESC: We weren't expecting so much rain. Our main shop got flooded. The equipment is starting to rust, and we need to get it up and running ASAP. We're on a deadline here! David should've checked the roof properly when I told him...
US_14_01_STARTING_WORK_REW: Awesome! That solves our leaky roof issues. Should we upgrade the drainage system just in case? Anyway, we're all set to start working with wood now. Thanks for the help!
US_14_01_CASTLE_UNDER_LOCK_DESC: Ugh, we thought we had everything all set up — the flags, the display cases, the tables and chairs — but we forgot the most important thing: entertainment and food! Good thing the crops are ready for harvesting, and there should be enough hay to go around. We'll pay you well if you help out.
US_14_01_CASTLE_UNDER_LOCK_REW: Planning hiccups are the worst. But thanks to you, we're ready to get this party started!
US_14_01_PROBLEM_IN_THE_PORT_DESC: Stop right there! The port's closed. We've run out of fuel, and our ship's engine is out of whack. I guess you could help us out if you need to cross. We do have a crane...
US_14_01_PROBLEM_IN_THE_PORT_REW: Let's see... we've got the fuel, and the engine's repaired. That means we can get you to the other side now. An eye for an eye, a delivery for a delivery!
US_14_01_PIPES_DELIVERY_DESC: We're the only ones who have access to water extraction equipment in the area. Heavy use has caused a lot of wear and tear in the pipes, so they need to be replaced right away. Our usual driver's on sick leave, which is why we need your help delivering supplies.
US_14_01_PIPES_DELIVERY_REW: Let there be water! All systems go. The pressure is up, and the pipes aren't leaking! Thank you for agreeing to this. We've got a stable water supply again! Here, you've earned this.
US_14_01_REPAIR_PROBLEM_DESC: I dunno what we were thinking when we started these repairs... We just went in and did the job, but when it was time to leave... We got stuck. Well, we managed to get out, but the construction rig is still there. Maybe you'd be able to pull it out.
US_14_01_REPAIR_PROBLEM_REW: Way to go, man! Your spatial thinking was genius. You don't happen to be a Tetris fan, do you? Just kidding! You did an amazing job. Here's a cut of our pay. You've earned it.
US_14_01_MAKE_HASTE_SLOWLY_DESC: I should've known not to take that shortcut... Hey buddy, I see you've got a powerful ride there. Could you tow my baby for me? She's all alone out there, covered in mud...
US_14_01_MAKE_HASTE_SLOWLY_REW: There she is, my sweetheart! You had to stay in that awful place and wait for the nice man to get you out, you poor thing. No worries, we'll give you the TLC you need! Thanks for the help, bud! This is for you.
US_14_01_ELEMENTAL_POWER_DESC: We just wanted to see the beautiful countryside, but the power of nature proved to be too much for us. We're okay, but our trailer has been washed away to God knows where. All our worldly possessions are in there... Please help us get it back, or this'll be the end of the road for us.
US_14_01_ELEMENTAL_POWER_REW: Phew! Camera — checked. Hard drive — checked. Can't seem to find my ID... Eh, whatever, I'll just get a new one if it comes to that. Thank you so much! You're the best.
US_14_01_PROBLEM_WITH_TURNING_DESC: Jeez, these sharp turns are driving me nuts. I'm not getting behind the wheel again — that's where you come in. Could you please deliver this tank to the farm and then get the truck to the garage?
US_14_01_PROBLEM_WITH_TURNING_REW: I really appreciate your help. Here's your reward. I just paid for a month-long extreme driving course. Maybe I could still learn how to take a sharp turn. I might even become a stunt driver!
US_14_01_SHARP_TURN_DESC: I was driving home from Aunt Amelia's when I lost control and went off the road. I managed to get out, but my car's still lying at the bottom of the lake. Could you please get it out for me? Maybe it's still salvageable.
US_14_01_SHARP_TURN_REW: Okay... the damage doesn't look too bad, plus I've got some fish in the back seat. Here, take this for your efforts.
US_14_01_UNPLANNED_UNLOADING_DESC: This is a disaster! I can't possibly stop the presses and leave people without our local news! The driver shouldn't have taken that mountain route. Now our cellulose is scattered all over the place and is at risk of rotting. I need it brought here. Would you help me?
US_14_01_UNPLANNED_UNLOADING_REW: Thank God, the cellulose has barely suffered any damage! We're going to start printing right away so the locals can get their fresh newspapers as soon as possible. And here is your remuneration!
US_14_01_DIRT_FROM_PAST_DESC: Everyone has secrets... even the ground. Our geologists have discovered a small chemical dump site in an old mine. That stuff isn't safe - it's poisoning the soil. Could you take it to the garage for now? We'll have it processed in a bit.
US_14_01_DIRT_FROM_PAST_REW: Well done! We've managed to keep the level of pollution within reasonable limits. Our team is analyzing the aftermath as we speak. That's good enough in my book, so here's your pay!
US_14_01_LOSS_IN_RIVER_DESC: Whatever possessed me to take my boat out in this weather? I'd never have dropped the crates if the water wasn't so choppy. Luckily the supplies don't weigh a lot, so the crates are just floating there. Hey, could you see if some of them have washed up ashore?
US_14_01_LOSS_IN_RIVER_REW: Three whole crates? Lucky me! It's better than nothing, I guess. Thanks for all the help. Here's your reward.
US_14_01_PRODUCTION_ERROR_DESC: This is the first time someone's returned our goods under warranty. We value our reputation, so we've decided to recall the whole batch of wood. I hope they still give us a five-star review. Could you deliver the batch for us?
US_14_01_PRODUCTION_ERROR_REW: Yeah, a lot of the planks got warped — maybe because of the heat. Some of them are actually twisted. We've already prepared a replacement batch and can't wait for the reviews to start coming in! Oh, here's your pay!
US_14_01_WATER_TREATMENTS_DESC: Our pool is very popular with the tourists when it's hot out. The thing is, they cut off our water due to pipeline maintenance. We do need to fill the pool somehow. The only solution I see is getting it delivered in a tank. Please hurry before people start complaining!
US_14_01_WATER_TREATMENTS_REW: Ah, I feel so much better now that our problem is solved. Water really is the stuff of life! Here's your fee and a free pass to our pool. You've earned it!
US_14_01_FORTRESS_CENTURY_DESC: With so little time before the construction deadline, our drivers tend to forget that the weight capacity of a trailer is limited. Because of this, some trailers get stuck along the way. I guess we'll just have to tow them... you in?
US_14_01_FORTRESS_CENTURY_REW: We're lucky this castle is in better shape than the Bohemian ones back in Wenceslaus IV's days. But the restoration is nowhere near complete. Well, you did your part, so here's your reward.
US_14_01_FUEL_PROBLEMS_DESC: Gas-guzzling generators are a thing of the past. We need to think of the future. Write this down: we need a fuel tank brought from the gas station, and don't forget to tell them that Gilbert sent you! What was I talking about? Oh, right. We've hit rock bottom!
US_14_01_FUEL_PROBLEMS_REW: You did it without any unnecessary incidents and in a relatively timely manner. Anyway, I'd like to thank you on behalf of the whole tourist camp!
US_14_01_LET_THERE_LIGHT_DESC: The lighthouse keeper insists that the light went out when some bearded man in a robe was trying to use his lighter. I think the bulb's just dead. We need a replacement from the trailer, and some tools too... Could you make the delivery?
US_14_01_LET_THERE_LIGHT_REW: Good job. It's nice to have the lighthouse on again. By the way, there was nothing wrong with the old bulb. It just wasn't working for some reason. Must be magic... Well, here's your pay, and thank you for the help!
US_14_01_URGENT_PHOTOS_DESC: We're in luck! "Architecture Monthly" is running a Medieval Castles photo competition. There's a submission deadline, so we don't have much time for the photo shoot. Could you drive to a couple of places and take some pictures real quick?
US_14_01_URGENT_PHOTOS_REW: You're so efficient! Here's a little something for your trouble. You can leave the rest to me. I'll have the pictures processed, do a bit of color correction, and submit them to the magazine.
US_14_01_CITY_RACING_DESC: Hey, buddy! We've got a prize pool set up for an upcoming race. Just keep in mind that half of the track is concrete, the other — dirt. You gotta work the throttle on this one, but don't overdo it! You wouldn't want to run yourself into a ditch wheels-up.
US_14_01_CITY_RACING_REW: That was crazy! You sure did great! And here's your prize! Remember to fasten your seatbelt and drive safe out there!
US_14_02_BUILD_CASTLE_01_DESC: A tower on one of our historical landmarks collapsed because of the rain. We're requesting help delivering construction supplies. The mayor's office will reward you handsomely.
US_14_02_BUILD_CASTLE_01_REW: Majestic, isn't it? Our restoration experts have really outdone themselves. You can't even tell where the old stones end and the new ones begin! You did a good job too, so here's your reward.
US_14_02_SCOUTING_CASTLES_DESC: The authorities need us to check three ancient castles in the area to determine the best routes after all this bad weather. The job's simple and pays well. Are you up for it?
US_14_02_SCOUTING_CASTLES_REW: We appreciate your input. The existing tourist routes will need to be altered to account for these new circumstances. Your comments on road quality will also be taken into consideration. Here's your fee.
US_14_02_SCOUTING_EXPLORER_DESC: Our company has been made aware of the additional fire hazards during the stormy season. We need you to inspect the area. Your efforts will be well compensated.
US_14_02_SCOUTING_EXPLORER_REW: Looks like the fields are located within a reasonable distance from the forest, and the surrounding vegetation doesn't exceed the height limit. As for the forest: the fire has been contained, and we've relayed the data to all interested parties. Great job!
US_14_02_FORREST_HELP_DESC: There's work to be done! We need living quarters and some equipment for our loggers. Please deliver everything on the list. We promise you a handsome reward.
US_14_02_FORREST_HELP_REW: The work season is finally here. We've provided our people with all the equipment and information they need. Here's hoping they've brought their willingness to work with them! And here's the payment I promised you.
US_14_02_HELP_FARM_DESC: The beavers' reaction to this bad weather was immediate. Don't get me wrong, they're pretty cool animals, but we've got enough on our plate as it is. Please help us deal with the flood damage on the farm. We'll pay you well.
US_14_02_HELP_FARM_REW: Beavers sure work hard. If our farmhands were as quick to react to the weather, maybe the place wouldn't be so wet. Thank you for being so efficient!
US_14_02_FORREST_BURNED_01_CONTRACT: Ashes to Ashes, Water to Soil
US_14_02_FORREST_BURNED_01_DESC: You helped us investigate the source of the fire earlier. Now we need to make sure all the fires have been put out. We've got a water delivery job for you. Interested?
US_14_02_FORREST_BURNED_01_REW: Great! Now that those blazes went up in smoke, we won't have any more major forest fires. Here's your cut, as promised!
US_14_02_FORREST_BURNED_02_DESC: Half the work's done, but we still need to deal with the burned logs. You'll need a special vehicle for this part. It's a decent job with decent pay.
US_14_02_FORREST_BURNED_02_REW: The burned trees have kicked up so much ash and soot that it's getting real hard to breathe in the area. I hope you remembered to wear a filter mask. But anyway, it's fine to take a breather now! You did a great job.
US_14_02_DELIVERY_LOG_01_DESC: Now that we're done literally putting out fires, it's time to tackle our orders. The first client needs us to take these logs to his lonely little house. He promises to pay well if we make it in time. Oh, and you're free to take our trailer to make things faster.
US_14_02_DELIVERY_LOG_01_REW: You're a master of your trade, my boy! No idea why the client would need so many logs, but sticking our noses into other people's business isn't part of the job. Here's your cut, and you can take the trailer too. We've ordered a new one with the money you earned us.
US_14_02_DELIVERY_LOG_02_DESC: The orders are still rolling in. This time, the guys from the stables are offering us a hefty sum to help build some stalls for their shiny new mustangs. They're gonna expand their horseback-riding operation. This is a top-priority order!
US_14_02_DELIVERY_LOG_02_REW: Now that's a lucrative job if I ever saw one. The client added a little bonus to thank us for our efficiency. It's all yours!
US_14_02_DELIVERY_LOG_03_DESC: Tourism is flourishing, which is always a good thing. It also means more work for us. We need to deliver some logs to the two new guesthouses. Chop-chop! The sooner you do it, the sooner they can start renting out the properties.
US_14_02_DELIVERY_LOG_03_REW: Great job! The client is pleased with the quality, which means the houses will be ready any day now. If you're tired of all this running around, consider renting one of the guesthouses for a vacation.
US_14_02_SERVICE_HOUSE_DESC: We need the service center upgraded as soon as possible. Since everyone is in such a rush to get ready for tourist season, things keep breaking, and it's the closest shop qualified to keep our equipment up and running. We're counting on you!
US_14_02_SERVICE_HOUSE_REW: We appreciate your efficiency. Please accept this reward.
US_14_02_SOLAR_PANEL_DESC: As meteorologists say, get the most out of good weather while it's here. Considering the rising cost of electricity and the recent sunny spell, we've decided to install solar panels in two areas. Now we just need to get them there. Do you think you're up for the job?
US_14_02_SOLAR_PANEL_REW: If everything goes according to plan, we'll be able to cut our electricity budget in half and boost morale, all in one fell swoop. As long as the meteorologists don't predict another rainy week, that is!
US_14_02_FARM_SOYA_DESC: You've proven to be a deft hand at potato farming. Do you think you could do the same with soybeans? The soil here is very fertile, not like the stuff on Mars. I think you'll be fine. So what do you say?
US_14_02_FARM_SOYA_REW: Good job, my friend. The soybean crop has doubled compared to our last harvest. Here's your pay!
US_14_02_FARM_POTATO_DESC: See that field? Grab a tractor and plow it. Get that done, and I'll give you some seeds. Or don't—but then you ain't gettin' jack. You sow the seeds, you get potatoes. Bring those to me, and I'll pay you. Now move it! I haven't got all day.
US_14_02_FARM_POTATO_REW: Good job! You did great with the field and the repairs. The harvest is way bigger than expected. You can have our trusty beast of a tractor for that!
US_14_02_DELIVERY_POTATO_DESC: With great power comes great responsibility. After harvesting a bumper crop like that, we need to get the bagged potatoes delivered to our customers. People need our potatoes, so everyone's counting on you.
US_14_02_DELIVERY_POTATO_REW: We can rest easy knowing that our goods have found a market and people won't have to go hungry. And good deeds should be rewarded. Here's your pay, plus a bag of potatoes for your personal use!
US_14_02_ROCKS_01_DESC: Dammit, I've just about had it with this mountain! So many landslides... a simple cleanup won't cut it anymore—we need to make the road safe. Could you deliver some concrete blocks and other supplies to the site?
US_14_02_ROCKS_01_REW: Okay, we've cleaned up the landslide and reinforced the mountainside. That should solve our landslide issues. Thanks for your help!
US_14_02_ROCKS_02_DESC: What a nightmare! This cliff's been here for hundreds of years. Now look at all the rubble and broken pitons all over the road... it's been 127 minutes since the emergency service was called, but those guys don't seem to be in a hurry to do their job. Could you help us out?
US_14_02_ROCKS_02_REW: I'm so glad you agreed to help! That was a life-or-death situation, so you really saved the day. Thanks a bunch!
US_14_02_TRUCK_01_DESC: Hi there. Name's Frank. I'm from Ireland. I took a delivery job in the area but got a bit distracted while driving. Ended up damaging my engine. The plugs are fine, the distributor connector too. But the T-belt got jacked up. Could you give me a lift to the warehouse?
US_14_02_TRUCK_01_REW: Thank you for the help. Here, take this part of my insurance money for your time and effort. Well, good luck out there!
US_14_02_BUILD_BRIDGE_01_DESC: Well, this job requires extra speed and dedication. We need to have this bridge fixed, like, yesterday. Considering the urgency, I guess performing a barrel roll on a plane would be a more reasonable demand than this. But, uh... could you help?
US_14_02_BUILD_BRIDGE_01_REW: Great job! That was fast, and, more importantly, there were no incidents. Here's a little something from our team.
US_14_02_CAMP_RUNNER_DESC: Everyone needs support in these trying times, especially the unlucky tourists. Please deliver a relief package to their camps. You'll be compensated fairly.
US_14_02_CAMP_RUNNER_REW: Despite the weather, the tourists are still going to have good memories about their trip. It's all fine as long as everyone's fed, warm, and has a place to sleep. A full belly and a good night's sleep make everything much more bearable. Thank you for taking care of those people.
US_14_02_SCOUT_RIVER_DESC: The local geologists are requesting a water-level survey of our crossings. We need you to visit all the sites. You'll be compensated.
US_14_02_SCOUT_RIVER_REW: What curious readings... well, thank you for your help.
US_14_02_TRUCK_02_DESC: Greetings, traveler! I decided to take a shortcut across these mountains, but a pile of rocks and some puddles landed my vehicle in deep water. It's a truck, not a submarine, so now I'm left waiting for someone to rescue me.
US_14_02_TRUCK_02_REW: My hero! Thank you. My name's Nemo, in case you were wondering. Here's my number. Just make sure not to lose it. And please accept your reward.
US_14_02_TRUCK_03_DESC: Wooo, that was quite a race! I'm still out of breath, to be honest. Uh, could we keep the details of this rally between us? And I'd appreciate some help with my car. As you can see, you win some, you lose some...
US_14_02_TRUCK_03_REW: I'm so glad you volunteered to help! You deserve this car way more than I do. Oh, and take some of my winnings too. You've earned it.
US_14_02_TRUCK_04_DESC: Who could've known the road right next to the fields would get ruined like this? It's never happened before, but here we go again! Sheesh... mind helping an old farmer out of the dirt? I'll make it worth your while, I promise!
US_14_02_TRUCK_04_REW: I lucked out with you, bud! That was no job for any old tow truck. Thanks for helping me out!
US_14_02_DELIVERY_HELP_01_DESC: I'm a hunter. A lot of my snares are in a sorry state, and I also need some parts for my car. We're not going anywhere until we get the supplies. I'll compensate you if you help me out.
US_14_02_DELIVERY_HELP_01_REW: Thanks a lot. Now it's actually open season. I hear there's a bear couple around here called Grizzly and Reindeer that go everywhere together. Now that I'd like to see! Oh, and here's your fee.
US_14_02_WATER_TANK_DESC: This is a disaster! Due to the lack of rain, the water levels in the region are so low, we're on the verge of a drought! How are people supposed to farm like this? To make matters worse, the pipeline broke down too. We need water right away! Please, we'll make it worth your while!
US_14_02_WATER_TANK_REW: Thank you so much for saving the area from a drought. Now both people and plants have enough water to go on. Here's your fee, just like we agreed. You're our hero!
US_14_02_SCOUT_TRAILER_01_DESC: I just wanted to spend the weekend high in the mountains, but ended up stuck in the mud instead. I've spent all this time waiting for someone to help me get my trailer back home... oh! Maybe you could do the job?
US_14_02_SCOUT_TRAILER_01_REW: You driving by was a true stroke of luck! I should try this route again when it gets a bit drier. Here, this is for you.
US_14_02_SCOUT_TRAILER_02_DESC: Our fuel delivery driver decided to take a shortcut through the mountains, but lost his way and got his tank stuck in a crevice. We had to do an emergency evac for the guy, but his tank is still up there. Could you tow it here for us?
US_14_02_SCOUT_TRAILER_02_REW: Now that's what I call a job well done! Thanks for getting the tank back. Not like this will affect our prices, but at least there won't be any fuel shortages. Here, you can take part of the driver's pay.
US_14_02_TRAILER_01_DESC: The loggers borrowed our service trailer, and now we can't keep up with all the maintenance work at the port. And, as luck would have it, all our drivers are busy! Could you do us a solid and get that trailer back?
US_14_02_TRAILER_01_REW: Yeah, we definitely didn't sign up for this. Well, we can't go rocking the boat, since we're gonna need their logs soon. You were a big help. Here, you've earned this.
US_14_02_DELIVERY_HELP_02_DESC: Electricity costs way too much around here, so I prefer to heat with firewood. I've got a pretty big stockpile for now, but it might not be enough to get me through the winter. Could you deliver a couple of logs to me?
US_14_02_DELIVERY_HELP_02_REW: This is very nice wood! Long-burning and great for heating. I just hope the logs won't get TOO dry and fall apart by winter. Well, here's your pay, anyway!
US_14_02_TRAILER_02_DESC: Our hay reserves have started to rot because of the flood, and the driver who was supposed to get us a fresh batch got stuck halfway here. We need to act fast to keep our people fed and our animals healthy. Could you give us a hand?
US_14_02_TRAILER_02_REW: You're a life-saver! Thanks to you, we won't have to slow down our meat and milk shipments, and the animals won't have to go hungry. Oh, and you can take the trailer if you want. We'll buy a new one with the proceeds.
US_14_02_RACING_DESC: Hey, buddy! We hold amateur races here. The last racer was in such a rush to win, he drove off the mountain right after he finished. He did set a record, though. You up for beating it? Safely, of course.
US_14_02_RACING_REW: Whoa, you left everyone else in the dust! Gotta wait for it to settle now. Good thing you didn't cause a landslide, right? Well, here's your prize!
US_14_02_DELIVERY_CONT_DESC: Hi! I'm Daggett. Me and my brother Norbert got tasked with returning a haystack those thieving beavers stole from the forester's house. So here's the plan: I call for help, Norb watches the stack, and you help with the delivery. Sound good?
US_14_02_DELIVERY_CONT_REW: Way to go! You're so fast, man! Here's your reward. Keep in mind, though, those angry beavers are always after new haystacks.
US_15_01_CONSTRUCTION_FACTORY_CONTRACT_D: We still need two shipments of concrete slabs and four shipments of brick to finish our metalworks, according to the budget. But, once that's done, it'll be time to kick back and relax! Could you grab us something to drink while you're at it?
US_15_01_CONSTRUCTION_FACTORY_CONTRACT_R: Hey, great job out there. The metalworks is good to go, which means that we can have us a nice cinq à sept tonight. Thanks for helping out!
US_15_01_PLATFORM_CONSTRUCTION_01_CONTRA: Hey there! Name's Jim. I'm in charge of building an oil rig here, and that's no mean feat. We could really use your help. To start off, could you check why in the world the crane trailer's not here yet?
Great news! We're making progress, and the team is busy assembling the tower. Here's your pay. We'll keep in touch!
US_15_01_PLATFORM_CONSTRUCTION_02_CONTRA: The next step is moving the drill unit and some other equipment from the old quarry. Fair warning: roads are covered in ice, and a little birdie told me there are tons of cameras around. Don't get a speeding ticket.
Great, my people have already received the goods and are prepping them for work. Here's your pay. Don't go anywhere, okay? We'll call you back.
US_15_01_PLATFORM_CONSTRUCTION_03_CONTRA: Okay, we've got one last but very important thing to take care off—get an offshore mooring anchor made. The new metalworks will need some special containers to complete that order, and that's where you come in. Hurry up, okay?
Nice, they're casting the anchor as we speak! At this rate, we should be able to meet the deadline just fine. Keep up the good work, and here's your pay!
US_15_01_PLATFORM_CONSTRUCTION_04_CONTRA: We're in the home stretch now. That giant anchor needs to be moved to the port for rigging to the platform. It's a tricky job, so it'll take a hot minute. Be extra careful with this one, all right? We're counting on you.

Bingo. And off it goes to get that black gold for us! I hope the new platform can deliver. Anyway, congratulations! Our company would like to thank you for all your efforts. As for me, I think a Fiji vacation is in order!
US_15_01_LIGHTHOUSE_SUPPLY_01_CONTRACT_D: Hey there. I'm Clint, the lighthouse keeper. Please help a guy out! Some important cargo got stuck in a gorge on its way to the lighthouse. It's not going anywhere unless someone gets it out, and I need it to keep the lighthouse running. Could you lend me a hand?
US_15_01_LIGHTHOUSE_SUPPLY_01_CONTRACT_R: I appreciate the help, bud! The lighthouse will be A-okay thanks to your efforts, and I should be able to ensure the safety of ships in our rough waters. Here's a little something-something as a token of my gratitude.
US_15_01_LIGHTHOUSE_SUPPLY_02_CONTRACT_D: Hello! I'm back with another request now that the lighthouse has been patched up. I had some parts delivered to the port, and I'd appreciate it if you could get them for me. The lighthouse restoration efforts are still a work in progress.
US_15_01_LIGHTHOUSE_SUPPLY_02_CONTRACT_R: I really appreciate all your help. Restoring the lighthouse would've been impossible without your assistance. Trust me, I'm not just being nice when I say you're doing important work here. Here's your reward.
US_15_01_SHIPBOARD_SUPPLY_02_CONTRACT_DE: Hey, friend! We need your help at the docks. Our boss, Mr. Cameron, told us to hurry up and fix a ship in the dockyard, but we're all out of spare parts. Could you give us a hand and deliver a batch of parts from the docks?
US_15_01_SHIPBOARD_SUPPLY_02_CONTRACT_RE: Phew, thanks for the save. Fixing a behemoth like that takes a titanic effort when the job's time-sensitive! Your quick help lets keep on schedule. Much obliged!
US_15_01_RESTORE_ROUTE_CONTRACT_DESC: Hello there! We have a major issue on our hands—an avalanche damaged the bridges next to the mountaineering center, cutting it off from the rest of the world. We need to restore that transport link ASAP if we want any chance to get supplies and save the people out there. Help us out, eh?
US_15_01_RESTORE_ROUTE_CONTRACT_REW: Thanks for all your help! We've managed to restore communication, so getting supplies or contacting someone outside the mountaineering center should no longer be an issue.
US_15_01_PIPELINE_01_CONTRACT_DESC: Hey buddy! We need your assistance ASAP. There's a damaged pipe in our area that needs replacing. Any chance you'd be willing to make the trip and get us a new pipe? We'd really appreciate it.
US_15_01_PIPELINE_01_CONTRACT_REW: Thanks for your help! You came through for us when we really needed it. Things would've turned out much worse if it weren't for you. Here's hoping this payment is a suitable enough reward.
US_15_01_PIPELINE_02_CONTRACT_DESC: Hey bud! We're in big trouble. One of the pipelines is badly damaged, to the point that it could leave the locals without any water unless we replace the leaky part. I've been told you're a great driver. Could you handle the delivery before it's too late?
US_15_01_PIPELINE_02_CONTRACT_REW: Thanks for getting us out of that jam! You've prevented a proper disaster here. Dunno what we would've done without you. We'll be in touch if anything like that comes up again.
US_15_01_BUILDING_BRIDGE_01_CONTRACT_DES: I'm the deputy head engineer and the construction crew chief on this site. You can't pass through here, so you'd better go back. But, if you're actually willing to help, please bring us some cements and metal beams. We can't do anything without them.
US_15_01_BUILDING_BRIDGE_01_CONTRACT_REW: They say help's never late, nor is it early. It arrives exactly when it's needed. And I reckon they're right, considering your perfect timing! The whole crew says thanks, and here's your well-deserved reward!
US_15_01_BUILDING_BRIDGE_02_CONTRACT: The Woes of a Construction Worker
US_15_01_BUILDING_BRIDGE_02_CONTRACT_DES: Hey there, traveler! We urgently need some construction materials to finish this bridge. Could you get them for us? I hear you've got the skills, and I'm sure things will go a lot faster with your help.
US_15_01_BUILDING_BRIDGE_02_CONTRACT_REW: Thanks for your help with the bridge! We would've probably missed the deadline if it weren't for you. Hell knows when the project would've been finished at that rate. We appreciate your efforts, and I'm not just saying that. Here's a little something to show our gratitude.
US_15_01_BUILDING_BRIDGE_03_CONTRACT_DES: The authorities have already dispatched someone to inspect our bridge and make sure it's up to code, as all key infrastructure should be. By the time he gets here, our bridge needs to be at 100%, so we don't have any time to lose! We need supplies.
US_15_01_BUILDING_BRIDGE_03_CONTRACT_REW: You could say that Harbor's latest bridge marks a new chapter in our region's rich history of ease of travel. The new road has increased this area's traffic capacity. Your input has been invaluable!
US_15_01_CONSTRUCTION_CLIMBERS_CONTRACT_: Welcome to our base! This place is gaining popularity by the day, which means we're going to need more fuel and supplies. But, as they say, the more the merrier, eh?

Great job! Thanks to you, lots of new tourists should be able to enjoy our mountain trails. And we're going to set them all up with food and accommodations. Here's your pay. Oh, if you ever feel like trying the best tourtière in the world, you know where to find us.
US_15_01_SCOUTING_SHORE_CONTRACT_01_DESC: Welcome to Harbor! Our company works with the Canadian Coast Guard, and they've requested an initial inspection of the coastline. We need an expeditor who knows what they're doing. All expenses are gonna be covered. You up for the job?
US_15_01_SCOUTING_SHORE_CONTRACT_01_REW: Nice work. The data's all logged and forwarded to the coast guard. We appreciate your skill and efficiency. Here's your pay, and be careful while driving on ice!
US_15_01_SCOUTING_SHORE_CONTRACT_02_DESC: We've received an emergency call from John Cabot, the owner of one of the trapped sailing vessels. He says the ice shifted, damaged the hulls of all three boats, so they're in urgent need of some parts. Can you handle the delivery?
US_15_01_SCOUTING_SHORE_CONTRACT_02_REW: Good job saving those three boats. Their glowing masts are a sight to behold at night—would've been a shame to lose that. Oh, Mr. Cabot and his buddies asked me to thank you on their behalf and give you this reward!
US_15_01_ROAD_ISLAND_CONTRACT_DESC: The only way to reach Tidal right now is via ice crossing, but it's just not safe. A pontoon would work much better. We're going to need two metal rolls and two bundles of long, wood beams.
US_15_01_ROAD_ISLAND_CONTRACT_REW: Way to go! Thanks to all your help, we should be able to build that pontoon crossing and secure a safe route for the locals. You're a real life-saver!
US_15_01_CABLE_TRAILER_CONTRACT_DESC: The oil rig needs a special flexible drillstem to run, and they have it ready at the new plant. We're also short on fuel for our tower. Could you get it for us?
US_15_01_CABLE_TRAILER_CONTRACT_REW: Great! The team received the drillstem and is about to start installing it. Meanwhile, the tower's all fueled up and ready to go! Thanks for lending a hand. Here's your pay!
US_15_01_SHIPYARD_SUPPLY_TASK_DESC: After carefully considering the fuel consumption rate, we've come to the conclusion that the oil platform is missing eight more fuel barrel pallets. Therefore, taking into account the interests of oil workers, we need you to deliver those barrels to us.
US_15_01_SHIPYARD_SUPPLY_TASK_REW: If you take shipping costs into account, fuel becomes an expensive resource on the open seas, which makes everyone value it all the more! But you've done your part, so here's your pay.
US_15_01_ROAD_BLOCK_01_TASK_DESC: We need some consumables and wooden planks to clear a rockslide in the eastern part of the area. The only way around it lies through the Forbidden Plateau. Be careful - they say whoever sets foot there will be cursed by evil spirits.
US_15_01_ROAD_BLOCK_01_TASK_REW: Benevolent spirits must be smiling upon us! We were able to clear the rockslide without anyone getting hurt. Still, I'd be careful while driving if I were you. You never know what could be waiting around the corner. Take this, for your efforts!
US_15_01_ROAD_BLOCK_02_TASK_DESC: To get rid of this rockslide, the workers are going to need two pallets of planks from the sawmill. You can get around the roadblock through the gorge down below, but watch out—there have been a few Sasquatch sightings in that area. Maybe the rockslide was his doing?
US_15_01_ROAD_BLOCK_02_TASK_REW: Great! Work goes much smoother when you have all the supplies. We've added extra reinforcements to the weak parts of the stonework, so even if Sasquatch does get up to some mischief, the most he'll be able to do is scatter some pebbles.
US_15_01_ROAD_BLOCK_03_TASK_DESC: Well, looks like we've got another rockslide. The team will need two crates of spare parts to clear the way. You can get there if you go straight over the mountain, but they say there are wolves in the area. Try not to lay on the horn if you don't want them to rip off your license plate.
US_15_01_ROAD_BLOCK_03_TASK_REW: Great, the rocks are gone, and the way's clear. Did you know the locals called wolves "waheela" and believed them to be vengeful spirits? Just saying, keep your head on a swivel on that mountain road. Anyway, the job's done. We can finally go our separate ways.
US_15_01_TRACK_DELIVERY_01_TASK_DESC: You're not gonna believe what just happened! I decided to take a shortcut across this lake, because I was so sure the ice could take the weight of my car—but it started cracking! Now I'm stuck, and I really need to get out before my ride goes under. Help me out, eh?
US_15_01_TRACK_DELIVERY_01_TASK_REW: Oh, thank God! I was literally on thin ice for a moment there. You saved both me and my car! I don't even want to think what could've happened if you didn't come along. Thanks for pitching in, and here's your reward!
US_15_01_TRAILER_DELIVERY_01_TASK_DESC: Hello! We had a trailer stocked with mountaineering equipment ferried over to the port, but it still needs to be delivered to our center. Which is not something we can do on our own, so that's where you come in. Help us out, eh?
US_15_01_TRAILER_DELIVERY_01_TASK_REW: Thanks a ton, bud! We couldn't have done it without you, but now we finally have everything we need for the upcoming expedition. You're a godsend. Oh, right, and here's your pay!
US_15_01_TRAILER_DELIVERY_02_TASK_DESC: Lots of machinery at the new plant needs fuel to operate uninterrupted. It's our job to provide it, and it's yours to safely get it to the plant. Mind the greasy roads, eh? And give it your all!
US_15_01_TRAILER_DELIVERY_02_TASK_REW: That was easy-breezy for a pro like you, eh? Kudos from our whole team for the great ice-driving skills you got there. And here's your reward!
US_15_01_CARGO_DELIVERY_01_TASK_DESC: Hello! We're almost out of fuel, so I need you to get me a couple of barrels to keep our clients well-supplied. It's not something we can handle on our own, unfortunately. Will you take the job?
US_15_01_CARGO_DELIVERY_01_TASK_REW: By saving our gas station, you've saved everyone who comes here! I think that deserves a good discount on our services. You know where to find us if you ever need it!
US_15_01_CARGO_DELIVERY_02_TASK_DESC: Hey there, traveler! I used to spend my winters down south, but this year, the urgent foundation repairs have kept me here. And I'm 65, I'll have you know. Hey, since you drive, could you get me some cement, eh?
US_15_01_CARGO_DELIVERY_02_TASK_REW: Ah, I'll be able to get through the winter just fine now that my house is better insulated. And I'll get to enjoy watching those junco snowbirds, too!
US_15_01_PANTOON_BRIDGE_01_TASK_DESC: Hey there, buddy! We've got a problem. The bridge isn't ready yet, but people still need to get to the other side somehow. A temporary crossing would do the trick—just need the supplies to build it. Could you get them to us if you have the time?
US_15_01_PANTOON_BRIDGE_01_TASK_REW: Well done, you beaut! That's what we call expertise! Here's your fee, and a little bonus for the efficiency.
US_15_01_HOUSE_BUILDING_01_TASK_DESC: Hey, did you notice our town's development efforts yet? We're building new houses for people, and we could use your help to deliver some planks. All our contractors refuse because of weather conditions. Give us a hand, eh?
US_15_01_HOUSE_BUILDING_01_TASK_REW: You've made great time! Thanks for being so fast and careful. We should be able to finish the houses and let their new owners move in soon. Please accept this as a token of our town's appreciation.
US_15_01_LOST_CARGO_01_TASK_DESC: It's a disaster! The incident with that container carrier can lead to regrettable pollution of the nearshore zone. The vessel was transporting some special chemicals. We need to search the coastline and retrieve the cargo ASAP!
US_15_01_LOST_CARGO_01_TASK_REW: Praise physics and Archimedes' Law that this cargo didn't sink! We're so lucky it washed ashore! You helped to prevent a major environmental disaster in the region. Here's your fee!
US_15_01_CARGO_PORT_01_TASK_DESC: Let's cut to the chase—we need two metal beams and four metal rolls delivered to the port. We'll pay you by the click. The job's nothing special, but it still needs done. You up for it?
US_15_01_CARGO_PORT_01_TASK_REW: Great, all the goods are accounted for. Let me sign your route order, and here's your receipt. Paid by distance traveled, just as promised. Keep up the good work!
US_15_01_PIPES_01_TASK_DESC: Whadda y'at, fella? Want a cut of that oil money? Well, all you have to do is deliver some large pipes for the oil line to our guys so that we can amp up extraction. It'll be worth your while, promise!
US_15_01_PIPES_01_TASK_REW: Great job, bud! That project wouldn't have gone anywhere without you. We should be able to increase throughput now, all thanks to you! Here's your cut, just as promised! Thanks for the service.
US_15_01_CRASHED_CARGO_TASK_DESC: What a disaster! Luckily, they've already evacuated the people, but our special delivery's still there. We have two undamaged containers by that shipwreck, and we need them delivered to the port despite any thin ice you might encounter. Will you take the job?
US_15_01_CRASHED_CARGO_TASK_REW: Your bravery and professionalism are commendable. Really, you deserve a medal, bud! Sadly, I can't award you one, but I can pay you in cold, hard cash. You've really saved the day!
US_15_01_TRASH_TASK_DESC: The event was a lot of fun! The clean-up, not so much. We've collected the garbage, but disposing of it without special tech doesn't seem feasible. Could you drop the containers off at the port if you've got a minute? We'd be much obliged.
US_15_01_TRASH_TASK_REW: Cool work, dude! The clean-up went so much faster thanks to you. With this place right as rain, we might as well throw another festival! You're our backstage hero.
US_15_01_METEO_DELIVERY_TASK_DESC: This weather station is in dire need of maintenance. The equipment is outdated, and some things really need to be replaced. We need supplies to get the station up and running again. Could you handle the delivery?
US_15_01_METEO_DELIVERY_TASK_REW: Great job! With the station in working order, we can finally monitor the local weather conditions. A good thing for everyone in the area, and it might even save someone's life. Your assistance has been invaluable! Here's your well-earned reward.
US_15_01_CONTEST_01_DESC: Hello! At this point, it's almost a tradition for our lab techs to race to Everton to alert the authorities about yet another Earth-bound comet discovered by Dr. Randall. Oh, there's another one! Would you like to join the race?
US_15_01_CONTEST_01_REW: Woah, you were fast! I'm sure you got there in time, even though the authorities won't process that data until much later. Besides, the holidays are coming—it's kind of a bad time for cosmic-scale issues. Anyway, have a bonus for being so lightning-fast!
US_15_01_CONTEST_02_DESC: I've got a job for you, and let me tell you upfront—it's not gonna be easy. A cargo ship got into an accident nearby. We gotta to act fast, before the haul sinks to the bottom of the sea. The government requested the goods be recovered ASAP.
US_15_01_CONTEST_02_REW: This is a big deal, buddy. If we'd have let this cargo go down, the town would've faced a lot of problems. But now, the goods are all accounted for, we'll take it from here. Thank you for the help. You've earned your reward!
