RU_17_02_MONASTERY_RESTORATION_DESC: I'm sure you know about this building restoration method that uses original materials. But we can't manage with just the old stones alone. Without modern materials, everything here will fall apart. Could you help deliver them?
RU_17_02_MONASTERY_RESTORATION_REW: Excellent! This temple is a mix of original and modern due to the use of ancient stones in the masonry combined with contemporary construction techniques. Beautiful and sturdy!
RU_17_02_CHAPEL_RESTORATION_DESC: Even though the local chapels don't have many parishioners, keeping them in good condition is our responsibility. So, each one needs a little TLC. Can you get us the supplies? It's not much...
RU_17_02_CHAPEL_RESTORATION_REW: Excellent! Now, we can be sure that every weary traveler can use the chapels to rest and learn about our ancestors while they're at it. Thank you for your help, and here's your reward. I'm sure this good deed will be remembered in your favor!
RU_17_02_SANATORIUM_RESTORATION_DESC: Tourist season is fast approaching, and interior work still isn't finished! We need resources to get the job done ASAP. The construction crew is standing idle. Can you help us out? We'll cover all the costs!
RU_17_02_SANATORIUM_RESTORATION_REW: Great! The construction crew has already started working, so we should be able to meet the deadline. Here you go. We appreciate your quick help, and I'm not just saying that!
RU_17_02_SANATORIUM_WATER_SYSTEM_DESC: We've already run all the water lines to the building, but we still need the industrial boiler delivered and installed. Without hot water, what kind of reviews will we get from tourists? Can you handle the delivery for us?
RU_17_02_SANATORIUM_WATER_SYSTEM_REW: Okay, now we just need the engineers to connect the boiler to our water supply system. Good job! Doesn't look like the boiler was damaged in transport, and we're on track to meet the deadline. Here's your payment!
RU_17_02_SANATORIUM_FOOD_DESC: It's also important to address the food situation. The kitchen is ready, all the appliances and furniture have been delivered, but we're short on freezers to store our food in. We need to get a couple of them delivered—could you help?
RU_17_02_SANATORIUM_FOOD_REW: Great, thanks for the timely delivery! Now that all the main systems are ready and functioning normally, we can start preparing for the guests' arrival. If you'd like to take a break, we can find a room for you, too. And here's your reward!
RU_17_02_HOLLY_WATER_DESC: Since our chapels don't have a direct connection to the water supply, they need their fresh water reserves topped up from time to time. Usually, local drivers handle that, but no one's available right now. Can you help out?
RU_17_02_HOLLY_WATER_REW: I'm sure the journey wasn't easy, given the location of each chapel. But now, thanks to your efforts, travelers who stop by certainly won't have to go thirsty. Here's your well-earned share for the hard work!
RU_17_02_SOLAR_ENERGY_CONTRACT_01: The Gifts of the Sun: Basics
RU_17_02_SOLAR_ENERGY_01_DESC: Welcome to the solar farm! We're in the home stretch now—just need to install 4 sections of solar panels to get this place humming. It's not back-breaking work, but the clock is ticking. We need metal beams and cement to get the ball rolling on the frames.
RU_17_02_SOLAR_ENERGY_01_REW: All right, good. The frames are up, so the foundation's been laid. These metal rails are built to last, so you can rest easy both about the structure's reliability and your pay. Here you go!
RU_17_02_SOLAR_ENERGY_CONTRACT_02: The Gifts of the Sun: Theory
RU_17_02_SOLAR_ENERGY_02_DESC: On to the next step—the solar panels. This cargo is quite fragile and expensive, so it needs careful handling. We need 8 pallets. My crew will stand by so they can start mounting them on the frame right away. We'll be waiting for your delivery!
RU_17_02_SOLAR_ENERGY_02_REW: Amazing work on your part! And the panels themselves are top-notch, too—once the protective film comes off, they look just like black mirrors! Alright, most of the heavy lifting is done, so we're almost there. In the meantime, here's your payment!
RU_17_02_SOLAR_ENERGY_CONTRACT_03: The Gifts of the Sun: Practice
RU_17_02_SOLAR_ENERGY_03_DESC: Curious about firing up the solar station, aren't you? Can't blame you—this doesn't happen every day. For us, the final piece of the puzzle is getting the new transformer installed at the substation to convert the power. Can you haul it over?
RU_17_02_SOLAR_ENERGY_03_REW: Excellent! Time to think about distributing power to the neighboring villages. By the way, the nearby guesthouse has already signed a supply contract with us. It's all thanks to you! Here's your reward!
RU_17_02_FERTILIZERS_DESC: Our regional agricultural company is offering support to local producers. As part of this support program, we've allocated a batch of soil fertilizer that needs to be delivered to the recipients. Will you take the job?
RU_17_02_FERTILIZERS_REW: Excellent! I see all the recipients have signed—that means everyone got the support they need. Now we just have to wait to reap what the beneficiaries sow. Thanks for your service!
RU_17_02_SEISMIC_HILLS_DESC: Hey there, pal! The local agricultural company has big plans for the fertile plots in the region. Thing is, we need to analyze the soil in the mountainous areas—check its structure and density. Feel like playing geologist for a while?
RU_17_02_SEISMIC_HILLS_REW: Yeah, you got some good data there. Not that the results are perfect, since vegetables don't grow on rocks. But there are still some usable plots here and there. Thanks for contributing to science, buddy!
RU_17_02_HYDRO_RESEARCH_DESC: You're well aware that, to expand further, the local agricultural company needs to analyze the local reservoirs' water composition, since it directly affects crop yields in the area. It's simple—4 buoys for 4 lakes. Can you handle it?
RU_17_02_HYDRO_RESEARCH_REW: Nice, all 4 buoys are connected to the system! I see each one is active and has already started collecting data. Now, we just have to wait for the system to run the analysis, but we can take it from here. Here's your reward!
RU_17_02_VILLAGE_AID_DESC: Great news! The local agricultural company is offering new grants to all interested farmers to boost their production volume. Many have already been approved for support—now it just needs to be delivered. And we need a carrier, someone like you.
RU_17_02_VILLAGE_AID_REW: A huge thank you from our farming community, and here's a reward from the agricultural company! Thanks for helping us develop the region. I'm sure that with support like this, we'll have a great harvest this year!
RU_17_02_FARM_WATER_DESC: What a disaster, our harvest is drying up! The hygrometer's readings have plummeted. The plants are starting to wilt, and our water reserves are running dangerously low. We need water. A lot of it. We'll pay well—just get it here, fast!
RU_17_02_FARM_WATER_REW: Perfect timing! This should be enough to revive our plants. Let's just hope this dry spell doesn't hurt the quality of the crop. Anyway, a deal's a deal—here's your payment!
RU_17_02_LUMBER_SUPPLY_DESC: Hey, traveler! Since you're passing through, care to lend a hand? We're running low on fuel, the portable generator's on its last legs, and we're short on spare parts, too. If you can haul this stuff over for us, we'll make it worth your while!
RU_17_02_LUMBER_SUPPLY_REW: Thank you, traveler—your timing couldn't have been better! You saved us from a halt in our quality timber deliveries. And with the spare parts, we can finally perform maintenance on all our equipment and ramp up the logging speed. Here's your reward!
RU_17_02_VILLAGE_SUPPLY_01_DESC: Welcome! We don't get many visitors in our village, especially driving rigs like yours. Since you're here—make yourself at home. By the way, could you help me patch up a huge hole in my cellar? Not for free—I'll pay you!
RU_17_02_VILLAGE_SUPPLY_01_REW: You've really done me a solid! Patching up the foundation in time guarantees that the house can keep standing for a couple more years! And the tools from the trailer made the repairs a whole lot easier. Here—for your trouble!
RU_17_02_VILLAGE_SUPPLY_02_DESC: Hey, pal, would you be interested in a side gig? I need to stock up on gasoline and sand. You never know when you might run out of fuel or need to build up a bank. So, can you get me the stuff? I'll pay!
RU_17_02_VILLAGE_SUPPLY_02_REW: I see you've brought me a couple tons of sand and several fuel barrels! Great, this'll come in handy—one of these days. I'll let it sit for now. And here's your payment!
RU_17_02_VILLAGE_SUPPLY_03_DESC: I don't get why people argue whether brick or wood makes a better house. Why not use both? I've already signed off on the blueprint for the new house—just need the materials delivered. By the way, care to help with the delivery?
RU_17_02_VILLAGE_SUPPLY_03_REW: Okay, boards—check, bricks—check. Now, what should I use for the walls? Could be brickwork, could be timber. Ah well, I'll figure it out. Here's your payment!
RU_17_02_TRUCK_HELP_01_DESC: Thank heaven I just replaced the brake pads on my car. They held up fine in the emergency, but the other parts weren't so lucky. Could you tow the thing and help me with the repairs? I'll make it worth your while!
RU_17_02_TRUCK_HELP_01_REW: My savior. And she's shining good as new! Thanks for not turning me down and fixing up my baby! As you can see, our roads are treacherous, so keep your eyes peeled. Here's the money for your trouble!
RU_17_02_TRUCK_HELP_02_DESC: The driver of our old truck got stuck on the way to the transformer area, apparently trying to take a shortcut. Of course, the company has already ordered us a better truck, but it's best not to waste old assets. Can you bring the truck back?
RU_17_02_TRUCK_HELP_02_REW: You've got a sturdy rig, having pulled our little truck out of the water. And thanks for the repairs and the fuel! Since it's going to be scrapped soon anyway, maybe you could take it off our hands for now? We're a bit short on space to keep two trucks here.
RU_17_02_TRUCK_HELP_03_DESC: I've taken this route more than a few times to show tourists around. But today luck wasn't on our side, and we got stuck. Looks like the road's completely washed out. Can you help me and my passengers? Get us back to the hotel? I'll make it worth your while.
RU_17_02_TRUCK_HELP_03_REW: Thanks, pal! If it weren't for you, we'd have been waiting ages for a tow given the staff shortage. Here—a well-earned reward for getting people out of a tight spot.
RU_17_02_TRUCK_HELP_04_DESC: The puddles don't seem to be frozen over. Weird—why did my car skid? I tried to slow down and straighten out before the turn but ended up in the ditch anyway, and there's a lake right behind it. Can you help me get out and fix up the car? I can pay!
RU_17_02_TRUCK_HELP_04_REW: I saw how much care you put into getting my car out and fixing it up. Thank you! You can keep it. I got a decent insurance payout, so I'm going to get myself another one—something better. The idea of driving the old one again just doesn't sit well with me.
RU_17_02_TRAILER_HELP_02_DESC: No one's safe from accidents like this. Thankfully, my truck made it through, but a big rock slammed into the trailer's side. You can see the result... Could you help me recover the cargo and pull the trailer out? I'll pay!
RU_17_02_TRAILER_HELP_02_REW: I had no idea you were so good with a crane! That was a masterclass. We're also lucky that the special cargo packaging is sturdy enough to prevent water damage to the boxes.
RU_17_02_NORTH_SCOUT_DESC: Listen, buddy, we've got a bit of a task on our hands. We need to scout around the logging base and look for quality timber. Our current spot's running dry, and we need to start planning a move.
RU_17_02_NORTH_SCOUT_REW: Perfect. Thanks for driving around and checking out the woods. I'll pass your findings on to management, let them decide where we should start logging next. Here, take this for your trouble!
RU_17_02_MOUNTAIN_SCOUT_DESC: Hey there, traveler! I've lived here for a long time, and with the agricultural company expanding in the region, all the roads are seeing more traffic, which isn't doing them any favors. Could you drive around and check what shape the mountain trails are in?
RU_17_02_MOUNTAIN_SCOUT_REW: Well, the fact that you managed to get around means the routes are passable. And, judging by your notes, there are no blocker obstacles or landslides—that's great news! Here's your reward for the good report!
RU_17_02_SWAMP_WOOD_DESC: Hold up there, sonny! Care to do an old man a favor? You're the only heavy rig driver around these parts. Could you bring me some construction boards? My old house is getting a bit drafty. I'll see you right—won't let kindness go cold.
RU_17_02_SWAMP_WOOD_REW: Well, I'll be! You've done this old man a solid, kid. Don't you fret about these loose boards—we've got boys in this village who were born swinging hammers. Here's your pay, straight and true. Now get on down that road—and may your wheels find nothing but smooth gravel ahead.
RU_17_02_VILLAGE_WOOD_TASK: No Food for the Wood Borers
RU_17_02_VILLAGE_WOOD_DESC: Ugh, if there's one thing I can't stand around here, it's these pests. The wood-boring beetles are multiplying like nobody's business, and that's bad for timber houses. If it's not too much trouble, could you bring us some solid planks for repairs?
RU_17_02_VILLAGE_WOOD_REW: Now that's a fine bit of timber... Looks like pine, judging by the grain. While you were gone, I went and got myself some proper beetle-repellent wood stain, too. This time, those little devils won't be feasting on my house! Thanks for helping me out. Here's something for your trouble.
RU_17_02_SCANER_DESC: Ah, a new face! We're trying to patch the hotel into the communication network, but the reception's weak and the frequency keeps jumping. Think you could lend us a hand? A good folding radar would sort this coverage issue right out.
RU_17_02_SCANER_REW: Nice! Just like we hoped, the signal's coming in clear now. And since this setup also works as a relay, nearby villages will get much better coverage, too. Well, thanks for your hard work and here's your pay—let's stay in touch!
RU_17_02_SEISMIC_SWAMP_TASK: The Secrets Buried in the Soil
RU_17_02_SEISMIC_SWAMP_DESC: Can't say why, but over the last few years the swamp's been spreading, and the water level's crept up a good half-centimeter on average. Even though the water's stagnant. Please check the geological activity for us—we'll make it worth your while.
RU_17_02_SEISMIC_SWAMP_REW: Well, you've managed to take some surprising readings. That means we've got our work cut out for us. At the very least, we need to track down that groundwater source. Thanks for your service! As agreed, here's your payment.
RU_17_02_SWAMP_RACING_DESC: Hey, buddy, have you heard the local tale about the hermit who still lives all alone out in the marshes? They say you might just run into him out there. Are you up for a ride? I bet you won't be coming back.
RU_17_02_SWAMP_RACING_REW: I take it all back—you're safe and sound and didn't even lose your car to the swamp! Here's the money I owe you. Looks like you've got nerves of steel, so if you ever feel like doing another run, just say the word—we'll set it up!
RU_17_02_MOUNTAIN_RACING_DESC: Hey, good to see you! The locals and I occasionally set up races along the mountain trails. If you're looking for a shot of adrenaline, you've come to the right place. So, care to test your skills? But be warned—it's pretty risky!
RU_17_02_MOUNTAIN_RACING_REW: Whew, now that was a solid start! You're clearly no rookie behind the wheel, but try to tread carefully going forward—those turns are sharp, and Lady Luck is fickle. If you're up for another round, just drop off the entry fee and put the pedal to the metal!
